channel.title: CrossWire
channel.description: Curated briefings from MOCR — Hermes, Tech, and Commanders

item.title: CrossWire — Morning Brief, July 24
item.description:
CrossWire: Hermes — July 24 Morning

🚀 Release Radar: Hermes Agent v0.19.0 — The Quicksilver Release

v0.19.0 dropped July 20 and it's a speed monster. ~2,245 commits, ~1,065 merged PRs, ~3,300 issues closed. The headline: first-turn time-to-first-token dropped ~80% across CLI, gateway, TUI, desktop, and cron. That cold-start "Initializing agent..." wait went from ~4.3s to ~0.9s. Desktop app got 20+ targeted perf PRs — 14× faster streaming markdown, virtualized diffs, snappy session switching. Reasoning streams live by default.

Beyond speed: smart approvals judge flagged commands automatically, durable delivery ledger ensures finished responses survive a gateway crash, Bitwarden/1Password native auth, and you can manage your Nous subscription from the terminal.

MOCR Relevance: The first-token speed improvement matters every session. The durable delivery ledger directly impacts MOCR reliability — no more lost completions if the gateway goes down mid-response. Smart approvals could simplify MOCR's approval workflows.

🛠️ Community Builds — New This Week

  • design-judge-skills (⭐321) — Evidence-driven agent skills for design award research, evaluation, matching, and entry writing. Strong debut.
  • frakio-work (⭐52) — Multi-agent collaboration workspace stitching Codex + Hermes + Arc together. "Frakio" = frak + I/O. The name alone earns a look.
  • hermes-improvements (⭐8) — Architectural enhancement suite: vector memory, adaptive soul, workflow classification for Hermes.
  • agent-console (⭐3) — Lightweight browser mission control for one Hermes agent: chat, sessions, cron, skills, voice. Very MOCR-adjacent concept.
  • hermespace (⭐3) — "Pocket workbench for Hermes Agent" — limited FOA desk, dual decode, skills+MEMORY fabric.
  • slashstack-skills (⭐3) — 11 MIT-licensed skills for coding agents: /preflight, /audit, /ship, /remember.
  • hermes-newton-operations (⭐2) — Profile-scoped native operations dashboard and bounded decision journal for Hermes.

📈 Gaining Popularity (This Month)

  • hermex (⭐908) — Native iPhone app for your Hermes agent. Taking off fast.
  • EvoForge (⭐101) — High-performance desktop agent workbench with evolutionary capabilities.
  • memmy-agent (⭐68) — Personal AI agent & local memory hub for Claude Code, Codex, OpenClaw, and Hermes.
  • mind (⭐58) — Persistent memory for coding agents: automatic capture, explainable recall, knowledge consolidation.
  • lyric-video-director (⭐43) — Hermes skill for typography-led lyric videos using GPT Image 2 and Gemini Omni Flash.

🔍 Trending Issues & PRs

  • #51610 — Config page search bar with Cmd+K shortcut. Nice QoL for dashboard navigation.
  • #70759 — Cross-session cwd race fix: adds lock to _task_env_overrides in terminal tool. MOCR relevance: Could affect us since MOCR runs concurrent gateway sessions.
  • #70758 — Tool defs cache race: adds lock to _tool_defs_cache preventing concurrent dict mutation from 10-thread gateway pool.
  • #70757 — Thread-local tool bleed fix: makes _last_resolved_tool_names thread-local to prevent cross-session tool name bleeding.
  • #56616 — pre_gateway_dispatch reply action — new hook point for gateway-level pre-processing.

📚 Worth Reading

  • Best Hermes Agent Skills in 2026 — Nanoskill's curated list of top community skills.

CrossWire: Tech — July 24 Morning

🍎 Apple

  • Claude Voice Mode gains Opus & Sonnet — Anthropic expanded Claude's voice mode to support Opus and Sonnet models, previously limited to Haiku. This means substantially more capable voice interactions — reasoning-grade answers while talking.
  • Apple v. OpenAI lawsuit reassigned — U.S. District Judge Edward Davila takes over after Apple declined magistrate judge jurisdiction. October case management conference vacated; settlement discussions and evidence exchange deadlines remain for Sept/Oct.
  • iPhone 18 Pro Max OLED price cuts — Apple pushing suppliers for 20% reduction as memory and component costs rise.
  • iOS 27 performance — 10 performance improvements actually worth noticing: smoother animations, faster AirDrop, better responsiveness across iPhones capable of running iOS 27.
  • Utah driver's licenses coming to Apple Wallet — signs found in Apple's backend. (MacRumors)
  • Lil' Finder Guy origin story — Apple art director Sofia Coelho shared early sketches and prototypes of the adorable mascot.
  • "The Dink" — Apple TV+ pickleball comedy starring... well, it's a star-studded sports comedy now streaming.

📱 Tech News

  • Google selfie login — Google now lets you log in with a selfie video for account access, AI Avatars, and age verification. Polarizing, but convenient.
  • Geekbench 7 released — Primate Labs overhauled benchmarks for CPU and GPU, with greater emphasis on ML and content creation workloads.
  • LG monitors pushing McAfee ads via Windows Update — Microsoft is investigating after LG monitors installed McAfee ads through Windows Update when connected to a PC. Third-party firmware bloat strikes again.
  • AI Kill Switch Act proposed — Bill would let DHS Secretary order shutdown of AI systems deemed rogue. Big implications for AI governance.
  • WhatsApp redesigned message bubbles on iOS — New bubble design rolling out per WABetaInfo.
  • PSA: Check your banking app — Important setting in your banking app (likely related to newly surfaced Apple Card feature).

CrossWire: Commanders — July 24 Morning

🏈 Training Camp Countdown

Camp is imminent: rookies report tomorrow (July 25), veterans report Tuesday (July 28). Full squad practices begin soon after.

Rookies to Watch

  • LB Sonny Styles (1st round) — Wore the green dot as defensive signal-caller during minicamp. Expected to compete with free-agent addition Leo Chenal for starting MLB and play-calling duties. Likely Day 1 starter for new DC Daronte Jones.
  • WR Antonio Williams (Clemson) — Nicknamed "Sade" by coaches (like the band's "Smooth Operator"). Earned rave reviews in OTAs. Could leapfrog Brown, Lane, and Jefferson to become the WR2 opposite Terry McLaurin.
  • C Matt Gulbin (6th round) — Will compete with Nick Allegretti for starting center spot on the O-line.
  • K Drew Stephens (undrafted, Iowa) — In a tight battle with returning veteran Jake Moody. Stephens appeared to have a slight edge during the offseason program.

Team News

  • John Riggins' #44 to be retired — The Hall of Fame running back's jersey will be retired at halftime of the Week 9 matchup against the Los Angeles Rams (Nov 8).
  • 2026 uniform schedule announced — The fully redesigned uniform closet has a schedule; will see each jersey in action throughout the season.
  • Ground game focus — GMFB's "burning question" is whether the revamped backfield can deliver on the offseason goal of getting the running game more involved.
  • Command Center Top 10 roster — Mitchell, Moss, and Smoot ranked the top 10 players on the current roster.

Schedule Snapshot

17 games remain. Season opens September 13 at Philadelphia. Key home games: Seahawks (9/27), Giants (10/11), Eagles (11/2), Rams (11/8 — Riggins ceremony), Cowboys (1/10).

Around the NFL

  • Rooney Rule at a crossroads — ESPN's deep investigation features 20+ coaches speaking about issues with the hiring process.
  • Cardinals edge Josh Sweat on PUP — Placed on physically unable to perform list due to knee injury after missing offseason activities for non-injury reasons.
  • 2027 draft class hype — ESPN asks if the much-anticipated 2027 class (quarterbacks, receivers, edge rushers) lives up to expectations. Teams have been looking ahead to this class for years.
  • Fantasy football draft guide season — ESPN's 2026 draft guide is live with rankings, mocks, and analysis.
